yields.note: model fit parameters: energy-transfer efficiency epsilon=0.22, membrane O2 diffusion efficiency epsilon_m=7.9e-4; protein-C:total-biomass-C ratio = 1:1.32

growth_records.cite: Inomura et al. A quantitative analysis of the direct and indirect costs of nitrogen fixation: a model based on Azotobacter vinelandii. The ISME Journal (2017). DOI:10.1038/ismej.2016.97. [PMC5315487]
growth_records.snippet: With growth rate of 1 per day, our Azotobacter study suggests a sweet spot of ~3% O2 saturation at which the organisms are neither oxygen-limited nor poisoned. ... At the lowest oxygen concentration (12 uM) there was a sharp change in the s
growth_records.notes: Modeling paper; quantitative sucrose-consumption fluxes (Cs vs D) are only in Figures 1/3/4 and not text-extractable as numbers, so uptake/secretion left empty. Growth-rate value taken from explicit text scenario mu=1 per day: mu_per_h = 1/24 = 0.0417 /h; doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.693/1 day = 0.693 day = 16.6 h; dilution_rate_per_h = D = mu at chemostat steady state = 1/day = 0.0417/h (0.00417 shown is the separately-stated transition D~0.1/day = 0.1/24 = 0.00417/h). Carbon source = sucrose (approximated as two glucose in model); nitrogen source = N2 (diazotrophic, N-free medium). O2 conditions 12-192 uM stated in text. Medium composition not given in this paper (derived from Kuhla and Oelze 1988b).

growth_records.medium: Burk medium (Burk-sucrose)

Comparison of poly-3-hydroxybutyrate (P3HB) synthesis by Bacillus cereus and Azotobacter vinelandii OP: effect of agitation on the accumulation and physicochemical properties of the biopolymer. Bioresources and Bioprocessing (2025). DOI:10.1186/s40643-025-00978-2. [PMC12718266]
growth_records.snippet: 50 (rpm) 48 (h) Q_PHB 1.18 ± 0.35 q_PHB 1.69 ± 0.52. P3HB minimal 0.07 ± 0.01 g L-1 at 50 rpm 48 h; pH dropped to 4.71 at 50 rpm.
growth_records.notes: P3HB volumetric Q_PHB and specific q_PHB experimental (Table 1B). P3HB 0.07 g/L (text). Burk medium composition fully stated (g/L). Initial pH 7.2 fell progressively; notable drop to 4.71 at 50 rpm from excreted organic acids (acetic, succinic, minor lactic) - acids qualitatively described but NOT quantified, so not listed as secretion rates. mu not reported/derivable. Temp 30 C. kLa 41.73 h-1 theoretical.

growth_records.snippet: the predicted growth using glucose (uptake rate of 0.33 mmol/gDW/h) as the sole carbon source was 0.1478 1/h and an alginate production rate of 0.25 mmol/gDW/h, agreeing with the experimental data (growth rate of 0.152 +/- 0.012 1/h and alg
growth_records.notes: A. vinelandii DJ alginate-production condition on glucose + ammonium. Experimental mu=0.152+/-0.012 /h and experimental alginate production rate 0.265+/-0.018 mmol/gDW/h, both cited FROM literature (Revin et al. 2018). td=0.693/0.152=4.56 h (calculated). Glucose uptake 0.33 mmol/gDW/h is a model constraint estimated from the experimental condition (is_experimental=false). Distinct from the glucose+ammonium record above (mu 0.0505, Clementi 1997) because this is a separate alginate-production growth study. Temp/pH not stated.

specific oxygen uptake rate qO2 (Table 1)
volumetric oxygen uptake rate OUR = OTRmax at steady state (dO2/dt≈0)

sec.method: MFA-derived relative carbon flux to CO2 (Table 2); not an absolute mmol rate

mfa.note: carbon usage to poly-3-hydroxybutyrate; highest of the three OTR conditions

growth_records.cite: García, Andres et al. Metabolic flux analysis and the NAD(P)H/NAD(P)+ ratios in chemostat cultures of Azotobacter vinelandii. Microbial Cell Factories (2018). DOI:10.1186/s12934-018-0860-8. [PMC5776761]
growth_records.snippet: the qG at the lowest OTR condition was 2.9 ± 0.2 mmol g−1 h−1 ... OTRs reached maximum values of 2.4 ± 0.03 ... for 300 ... rpm ... qO2 3.7 ± 0.16 ... The highest protein yield (0.15 gprotein gglucose−1) was obtained in the cultures develop
growth_records.notes: Chemostat D=0.08/h; mu=D=0.08/h (steady state). Doubling time td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.08=8.66 h (calculated). qG=2.9 mmol/g/h and qO2=3.7 mmol/gprot/h both experimentally measured (steady state, triplicate). OUR=OTRmax=2.4 mmol/L/h (volumetric; OUR=OTR since dO2/dt≈0). qAlg=0.44 mmol/gprot/h measured. P3HB accumulated intracellularly = 0.75±0.04 g/L (not a secreted product; no per-gram q reported numerically). CO2 (53.2% C-mol) and biomass/alginate/P3HB carbon splits are MFA-derived relative fluxes normalized to glucose uptake=100% (Table 2), not absolute mmol rates, hence is_experimental=false. NOTE: Fig 2b caption labels the axis 'specific sucrose uptake rate' and Ks values cited are for sucrose (from Díaz-Barrera ref 29), but the actual carbon source in this study's medium is glucose (10 g/L); text consistently reports 'qG'/glucose, so recorded compound=glucose. Yields from Table 1.

growth_records.notes: Chemostat D=0.08/h; mu=D=0.08/h (steady state). td=ln2/0.08=8.66 h (calculated). qG=6.12 mmol/g/h, qO2=20 mmol/gprot/h, OUR=OTRmax=14.3 mmol/L/h all measured. qAlg=0.61 mmol/gprot/h measured. growth_records.snippet: OTRmax reached values of 4.8 ± 0.28 ... for ATCC 9046 ... qO2 13.6 ± 0.4 mmol O2 g_prot-1 h-1, RQ 1.10, μ 0.09 ± 0.02 h-1, maximal alginate 3.6 ± 0.6 g L-1, qAlg 0.050, P3HB 58% (Table 1)
growth_records.notes: Table 1 experimental. qO2 is specific O2 UPTAKE (mmol O2 per g PROTEIN per h, not gDW). RQ=CTR/OTR=1.10 (implies CO2 secretion rate CTR=RQ*OTRmax=1.10*4.8=5.28 mmol L-1 h-1 volumetric). Doubling time td=ln2/μ=0.693/0.09=7.70 h. P3HB 58% is intracellular polymer fraction not a rate. Alginate max 3.6 g/L is a titer; qAlg 0.050 g g-1 h-1 is the specific secretion rate. Xmax not tabulated for this strain.

growth_records.cite: Pérez, Andrés et al. Tailoring 3HV Fraction in Poly(3-hydroxybutyrate-co-3-hydroxyvalerate) by Azotobacter vinelandii Through Oxygen and Carbon Limitation in Continuous Cultures. Polymers (Basel) (2025). DOI:10.3390/polym17192578. [PMC12526780]
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady state at 850 rpm -> qO2=64.4 (highest agitation, carbon-limited; highest CO2 fraction 93.8% and highest 3HV 36.40 mol%). mu=D=0.04; doubling_time=17.33 h. qO2 measured. Yields Table 2, carbon Table 1, biomass 1.01 g/L, P3HBV 0.33 g/L, 3HV 36.40 mol% Table A1 (850 rpm). qS/qVal Figure 3 only. Temp 30 C from inoculum.

growth_records.medium: Molasses medium
growth_records.cite: Jiang et al. Carbon Sources for Polyhydroxyalkanoates and an Integrated Biorefinery. International Journal of Molecular Sciences (2016). DOI:10.3390/ijms17071157. [PMC4964529]
growth_records.snippet: Molasses | A. vinelandii | 38.3 | 60 | P3HB | 1.4 | 0.12 [29]
growth_records.notes: REVIEW secondary value (Table 2, ref [29]). CDW 38.3 g/L, 60% P3HB, 1.4 g/L/h, 0.12 g/g molasses. Distinct source from the in-text Page fed-batch molasses record (0.575 g/L/h).

growth_records.medium: Beet molasses medium

sec.method: volumetric productivity (23 g/L over 40 h), in-text

growth_records.cite: Jiang et al. Carbon Sources for Polyhydroxyalkanoates and an Integrated Biorefinery. International Journal of Molecular Sciences (2016). DOI:10.3390/ijms17071157. [PMC4964529]
growth_records.snippet: After 40 h by fed-batch culture, the yield of PHA was 23 g/L with 66% PHA of cell dry weight. The productivity was 0.575 g/L/h, and the yield was 0.12 g PHA per gram of molasses consumed.
growth_records.notes: REVIEW in-text secondary value (Page et al. [22,27,28], fed-batch beet molasses, 40 h). PHA 23 g/L, 66% CDW, 0.575 g/L/h, 0.12 g PHA/g molasses. Consistency check: 23 g/L / 40 h = 0.575 g/L/h -> arithmetic matches.

growth_records.medium: Modified Burk's medium (sucrose carbon source, ammonium acetate nitrogen source)

up.method: specific sucrose uptake rate (SSUR) reported
calculated from SSUR 0.24 g/g/h / 342.30 g/mol * 1000 = 0.701 (assumes gDW ~ g biomass)
OTRmax (volumetric max oxygen transfer rate) via off-gas O2 analysis; not biomass-specific

product_yields.note: max alginate 3.1 g/L; biomass 4.2 g/L at end of cultivation

growth_records.cite: Lozano, Esteban et al. Oxygen transfer rate during the production of alginate by Azotobacter vinelandii under oxygen-limited and non oxygen-limited conditions. Microbial Cell Factories (2011). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-10-13. [PMC3056763]
growth_records.snippet: In the cultures conducted at 700 rpm, the specific growth rate was 0.25 h-1 and the biomass concentration at the end of the cultivation was 4.2 g L-1 ... the specific sucrose uptake rate was of 0.24 g suc g biom-1 h-1 ... maximal ... SAPR o
growth_records.notes: Condition: 5% DOT (non-oxygen-limited), 700 rpm. mu=0.25/h (Results); Fig 6 summary shows 0.23/h at OTRmax=100 (minor discrepancy, both reported values). doubling_time=ln2/0.25=2.773 h. Sucrose uptake 0.24 g/g/h converted to mmol/gDW/h: 0.24/342.30*1000=0.701. OTRmax 100 mmol/L/h is volumetric (per liquid volume), not per gDW; reported as O2 uptake proxy. Alginate titre 3.1 g/L at 24 h.

growth_records.medium: Modified Burk's medium (sucrose carbon source, ammonium acetate nitrogen source)

up.method: specific sucrose uptake rate (SSUR) reported
calculated from SSUR 0.44 g/g/h / 342.30 g/mol * 1000 = 1.285
OTRmax (volumetric) via off-gas O2 analysis

product_yields.note: max alginate 0.6 g/L; biomass 1.4 g/L; only 75% of sucrose consumed

growth_records.cite: Lozano, Esteban et al. Oxygen transfer rate during the production of alginate by Azotobacter vinelandii under oxygen-limited and non oxygen-limited conditions. Microbial Cell Factories (2011). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-10-13. [PMC3056763]
growth_records.snippet: at 300 rpm, mu was 0.15 h-1 with a maximal biomass concentration of 1.4 g L-1 ... only 75% of sucrose was used by the bacteria, calculating a SSUR of 0.44 g suc g biom-1 h-1 ... maximum of 17 mmol L-1 h-1 at 12 h
growth_records.notes: Condition: 5% DOT, 300 rpm (oxygen-transfer-limited 12-24 h). mu=0.15/h. doubling_time=ln2/0.15=4.621 h. Sucrose uptake 0.44 g/g/h -> 0.44/342.30*1000=1.285 mmol/gDW/h. OTRmax 17 mmol/L/h volumetric. Only 75% sucrose consumed (5 g/L residual at 24 h).

growth_records.medium: Modified Burk's medium (sucrose carbon source, ammonium acetate nitrogen source)

up.method: OTRmax (volumetric) via off-gas O2 analysis

product_yields.note: max alginate 2.1 g/L; biomass 2.7 g/L at 21 h; sucrose fully consumed by 24 h

growth_records.cite: Lozano, Esteban et al. Oxygen transfer rate during the production of alginate by Azotobacter vinelandii under oxygen-limited and non oxygen-limited conditions. Microbial Cell Factories (2011). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-10-13. [PMC3056763]
growth_records.snippet: A maximal biomass concentration of 2.7 g L-1 and alginate production of 2.1 g L-1 were reached at 700 rpm after 21 h of cultivation ... reach a maximum of 70 mmol L-1 h-1 at 15 h of cultivation
growth_records.notes: Condition: 0.5% DOT (oxygen-limited), 700 rpm. Specific growth rate not stated numerically in text for this condition (only plotted in Fig 6 vs OTRmax); left null. OTRmax 70 mmol/L/h volumetric. Nitrogen (ammonium acetate) depleted at 15 h limits growth. Biomass 2.7 g/L, alginate 2.1 g/L at 21 h.

growth_records.medium: Modified Burk's medium (sucrose carbon source, ammonium acetate nitrogen source)

up.method: OTRmax (volumetric) via off-gas O2 analysis; plateau 6-21 h

product_yields.note: max alginate 0.8 g/L; biomass 1.3 g/L; ~50% of initial sucrose not consumed

growth_records.cite: Lozano, Esteban et al. Oxygen transfer rate during the production of alginate by Azotobacter vinelandii under oxygen-limited and non oxygen-limited conditions. Microbial Cell Factories (2011). DOI:10.1186/1475-2859-10-13. [PMC3056763]
growth_records.snippet: in the cultures conducted at 300 rpm, the biomass concentration was of 1.3 g L-1 and the alginate concentration was of 0.8 g L-1 ... a maximal OTR of 6 mmol L-1 h-1 ... remained constant from the 6th to the 21st h
growth_records.notes: Condition: 0.5% DOT (oxygen-limited), 300 rpm. Specific growth rate not stated numerically for this condition (Fig 6 only); left null. OTRmax 6 mmol/L/h volumetric. Biomass 1.3 g/L, alginate 0.8 g/L; ~50% initial sucrose unconsumed.
